sections.text: SAP CORE REDESIGN PROGRAMME
Programme Management Framework — Milestones, Deliverables & Action Plan
UNESCORE 3.0 | Corporate IT Applications & Digital Transformation
Version 1.0 — April 2026
1. Executive Summary & Programme Rationale

This document translates the approved SAP Core Redesign roadmap into a concrete Programme Management Framework. It provides the milestones, deliverables, RACI responsibilities, and risk controls needed to execute the three-pillar redesign as a structured programme under CITO oversight.

sections.h2: The Three Pillars of SAP Redesign
sections.text: The Three Pillars of SAP Redesign

sections.h2: The Three Pillars of SAP Redesign
sections.text: Pillar 1 — Funds Management (FM) Re-normalisation

sections.h2: The Three Pillars of SAP Redesign
sections.text: SAP FM is currently used mainly for RP with a one-to-one coupling between projects and budget codes. This prevents flexible multi-fund structures and proper budget management. FM must be re-configured to cover all four sources of funds (RP, MCA, OPF, VC) with decoupled fund/project relationships and proper budget availability controls.

sections.h2: The Three Pillars of SAP Redesign
sections.text: Pillar 2 — Project System / WBS Level 4 Implementation

sections.h2: The Three Pillars of SAP Redesign
sections.text: PS/WBS is currently used inconsistently: mainly for VC, with multi-usage of fields and no Level 4 structure. The redesign imposes a standardised Level 4 WBS (thematic/geographic area down to activity-level for Output and Expenses), decoupled from source of funds, with proper SAP controls and full synchronisation with UNESCORE Core Manager.

sections.h2: The Three Pillars of SAP Redesign
sections.text: Pillar 3 — Business Partners (BP) Redesign / Grants Management Implementation

sections.h2: The Three Pillars of SAP Redesign
sections.text: The current Business Partner module lacks proper classification. Vendors, customers, donors, member states and meeting participants are not differentiated with appropriate typologies. The Grants Management (GM) module is non-existent. This pillar redesigns BP typologies and lays the foundation for GM implementation, enabling automated donor management and invoicing.
Approach
Rather than a big-bang implementation, the programme follows a phased, evidence-based approach:
2026 — Design & Validation: Impact analysis, sandbox data mapping, Go/No-Go gate at year-end
2027 — Implementation: Data harmonisation, full configuration, UAT, production deployment (FM & PS) aligned with 44 C/5 preparation
2027 - 2028 — Business Partners & Grants Management: BP redesign deployment, GM sandbox and go-live
2. Programme Milestones

The table below defines 18 key milestones across the three phases. Each milestone has a named primary deliverable, designated owner group, and a target quarter. Focal points from each owner group is to be defined. Milestones are grouped by phase and colour-coded accordingly.
Note: M7 and M16 (Design Phase Review are formal Go/No-Go gates. Progression to 2027 and 2028 implementation requires Steering Committee approval based on the design findings.
3. Deliverables — Definition & Required Content

This section defines what each key deliverable must contain. Deliverables serve as auditable proof of work and must meet the content criteria below to be accepted by the Steering Committee. SAP Steering Committee is composed of Senior Leadership from DBS, FIN, DBM and BSP. Sponsors must formally sign off each deliverable before the next milestone is triggered.
Governance Deliverables
Deliverables 1–4 must be completed in Q1 2026 before technical workstreams begin. They establish the governance backbone of the programme.
4. RACI — Roles & Responsibilities
The RACI below covers the core programme activities. Named individuals must be identified by workstream leads and documented in the Programme Charter within Q2 2026.
Key roles:
SAP Steering Committee (programme sponsor & escalation authority)
DBS SAP (technical delivery team — SAP configuration, ABAP, integration)
DBS EPM (Core Manager / UNESCORE integration)
FIN/DBM/BSP (business process owner for funds & projects)
FIN/AFAS / BSP (vendor & partner master data owner)
Project Officers (UAT participants; WBS adoption)
PMO (programme coordination, reporting, risk management)
Legend: R = Responsible (does the work)  |  A = Accountable (single sign-off authority)  |  C = Consulted (input required)  |  I = Informed (kept up to date)
5. Phased Action Plan
The following action plan details what technical teams and business teams need to do in each phase, enabling clear tracking of who is doing what and when.
Phase 1 — Design & Impact Analysis (2026)

sections.h3: Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)
sections.text: Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)

sections.h3: Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)
sections.text: Set up dedicated sandbox environment for redesign testing — isolated from 2026 operational work (Q2 2026)

sections.h3: Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)
sections.text: Document as-is FM configuration per fund source: fund types, budget profiles, availability controls, posting keys, RP/MCA/OPF/VC coverage gaps (Q2 2026)

sections.h3: Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)
sections.text: Perform FM impact analysis: model decoupling of project-budget tight coupling; identify transaction-level impacts on active projects (Q3 2026)

sections.h3: Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)
sections.text: Produce FM sandbox data mapping: configure sample fund structures for all 4 sources with actual data; document mapping rules and data quality gaps (Q4 2026)

sections.h3: Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)
sections.text: Document as-is WBS inconsistencies: field multi-usage, discrepancies, Core Manager sync, missing Level 4 structure (Q2 2026)

sections.h3: Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)
sections.text: Perform PS/WBS impact analysis: model Level 4 WBS structure; for all sources of funds; identify SAP control requirements (Q2 2026)

sections.h3: Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)
sections.text: Produce WBS sample mappings in sandbox: new RP, MCA, OPF, and VC project types at Level 4; validate Core Manager integration at WBS level (Q3 2026)

sections.h3: Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)
sections.text: Document as-is Customer (Business Partner) configuration; identify typology gaps; draft BP re-classification rules (donor, customer, vendor, member state) (Q4 2026)

sections.h3: Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)
sections.text: Prepare Design Review Report for Steering Committee Go/No-Go (Q4 2026)
Business Team Actions (FIN, DBM, BSP)
Provide named business owners and deputies for each pillar by May 2026 — no analysis can start without a confirmed business counterpart (Q2 2026)
Co-author FM to-be requirements with DBS SAP: define expected fund lifecycle rules, budget control expectations per fund source, reporting needs (Q3 2026)
Validate FM sandbox mappings: confirm that proposed fund structures reflect actual/adjusted budget management practices; identify edge cases (Q4 2026)
Co-author PS/WBS to-be requirements: define Level 4 WBS naming conventions, activity types, Output mapping rules, project specific needs (Q3 2026)
Identify pilots for WBS sandbox mapping exercises; provide actual project data for sample mapping (Q3 2026)
Validate WBS sample mappings: confirm Level 4 structure meets programme officer and reporting needs (Q4 2026)
Provide vendor/customer/donor master data typology breakdown to DBS for BP analysis (Q3 2026)
Participate in Go/No-Go Steering Committee review; confirm business readiness for 2027 implementation (Q4 2026)
Phase 2 — Implementation & Deployment (2027)
Technical Team Actions
Build FM configuration in Development environment based on Phase 1 design; apply fund lifecycle rules, budget profiles, availability controls (Q2 2027)
Promote FM configuration to QA; execute technical test scripts; fix defects; document configuration baseline (Q1 2027)
Build PS/WBS Level 4 configuration in Development; implement consistency controls; configure Core Manager synchronisation points (Q2 2027)
Execute integration test suite: FM <> Core Manager; PS <> Core Manager WBS sync; end-to-end budget flow from Core Manager to SAP commitment (Q3 2027)
Support UAT: provide test environment, assist with data setup, log and fix defects raised by business testers (Q3 2027)
Develop and execute cutover plan: data migration approach, go-live sequence, rollback procedure, cutover communication to all institutes (Q3-Q4 2027)
Execute production deployment; provide hypercare support for 12 weeks post go-live (Q4 2027)
Business Team Actions
Review and sign off FM and PS/WBS configuration documents before QA promotion (Q1-Q2 2027)
Lead UAT: design business test scenarios covering carry-forward, BOR processing, fund allocation, project creation, expense reporting; execute tests; log and accept/reject results (Q3 2027)
Provide formal UAT sign-off by named business sponsor before go-live approval (Q3 2027)
Prepare memos, participate in communication and change management: inform programme officers of new WBS rules; provide feedback on training materials (Q3 2027)
Train finance and programme teams using DBS-produced training materials; ensure all users complete training before go-live (Q3-Q4 2027)
Support post go-live stabilisation: monitor daily operations, report issues through agreed channel, participate in hypercare review meetings (Q4 2027)
Phase 3 — Business Partners & Grants Management (2028)
Technical Team Actions
Configure BP typologies in sandbox; test re-classification rules across all entity types; validate migration from SAP customer objects (Q3 2027)
Configure Grants Management module in sandbox; link to normalised FM and PS structures; test donor agreement lifecycle end-to-end (S1 2028)
Implement GM <> Core Manager integration for donor data, organisations and VC workflow (S1 2028)
Execute BP data migration to production; deploy GM (S1 2028)
Business Team Actions
Validate BP typology definitions: confirm donor, customer, vendor, member state classification rules are operationally correct (Q4 2026)
Design and validate Grants Management end-to-end process: from donor agreement in Core Manager to GM posting in SAP; confirm automation meets audit and compliance requirements (Q4 2027)
Support GM UAT and provide sign-off; confirm 44 C/5 budget cycle processes work end-to-end (S1 2028)
6. Programme Risk Register

The following key risks have been identified based on the assessment documents and programme context. The risk register must be reviewed monthly by the PMO and presented at each Steering Committee meeting.
Probability and impact are rated Low / Med / High. All High-High risks require an immediate mitigation action plan within 2 weeks of identification.
7. Programme Governance & Reporting
Governance Structure
SAP Steering Committee:
Monthly
Chaired by DIR/DBM, DIR/FIN, DIR/DBS, DIR/BSP(?)
Members include FIN – DBM - BSP key business counterparts (Amol, Anssi, Lionel, BSP tbd), DBS leads, PMO.
Reviews milestone status, risks, and Go/No-Go gates
Working Groups (per pillar : FM ; PS/WBS ; BP/GM):
Weekly or bi-weekly – to be adjusted according to the phase. Design phase recommendation is weekly.
DBS lead(s) + business focal point(s)
Reviews technical progress, resolves design questions, tracks deliverable completion
PMO:
CITO/PMO
Maintains consolidated programme plan, RACI, risk register, and status reporting. Issues monthly programme status report to CITO and Steering Committe

Reporting
Deliverable Quality Gates
All deliverables must pass a two-stage quality check before acceptance:
Technical review: DBS SAP lead confirms technical correctness and completeness against the acceptance criteria defined in Section 3.
Business review: Named business owner confirms the deliverable reflects actual business requirements and is fit for the next phase.
No milestone is marked complete until both reviews are signed off. The PMO maintains the Deliverable Tracker as the authoritative record.
Documentation & Methodology
Teams: Channel for communication and programme deliverables
Confluence: Design and technical documents (reference repository)
JIRA: 2-3 weeks Sprint planning, user stories, UAT and post go-live follow-up
